title: Systems and methods for signaling information for a mesh in omnidirectional media
abstract: To transmit information associated with an omnidirectional video, following three syntax elements are used. (See paragraph [0056] for details.) (1) "num_mesh_elements" syntax element specifying a number of mesh elements in the mesh box. (2) "explicit_mesh_id" syntax element indicating whether "mesh_element_id" syntax element is present in the mesh box. (3) "mesh_element_id" syntax element specifying an identifier to uniquely identify a mesh element within the mesh box.
claims: Claims ( 8 ) A method of determining mesh information associated with an omnidirectional video, the method comprising: receiving a mesh box specifying a three dimensional mesh consisting of one or more mesh element; parsing a number of mesh elements syntax element specifying a number of mesh elements described in the mesh box; parsing an explicit mesh identifier syntax element indicating whether a mesh element identifier syntax element is present in the mesh box; and parsing the mesh element identifier syntax element, which specifies an identifier to uniquely identify a mesh element within the mesh box, by using a value of the explicit mesh identifier syntax element for the number of mesh elements. The method of claim 1, wherein: different mesh elements in the mesh box are referenced by one or more tile mesh group entries, which define the relationship between a group of samples and a position on a mesh, and the one or more tile mesh group entries describes one or more rectangular regions and describes how one or more rectangular regions map to the one or more mesh element described by the mesh box. The method of claim 2, further comprising: parsing an eye present flag syntax element, specifying whether an eye syntax element is present, in the tile mesh group entry, wherein the eye syntax element specifies an eye associated with a rectangular region corresponding to a left eye or a right eye, in a case that a value of the eye present flag syntax element is present. The method of claim 1, wherein parsing an eye present flag syntax element, specifying whether an eye syntax element is present, in a mesh overlay, wherein the mesh overlay specifies that an overlay is displayed on indicated mesh element of a three dimensional mesh. The method of claim 4, further comprising: parsing an eye syntax element specifies a rectangular region corresponds to a left eye or a right eye, in a case that a value of the eye present flag syntax element is present. A method of signaling mesh information associated with an omnidirectional video, the method comprising: signaling a number of mesh elements syntax element specifying a number of mesh elements described in a mesh box; signaling an explicit mesh identifier syntax element indicating whether a mesh element identifier syntax element is present in the mesh box; and signaling the mesh element identifier syntax element, which specifies an identifier to uniquely identify a mesh element within the mesh box, by using a value of the explicit mesh identifier syntax element for the number of mesh elements, wherein the mesh box specifies a three dimensional mesh consisting of one or more mesh element. A device comprising one or more processors configured to: receive a mesh box specifying a three dimensional mesh consisting of one or more mesh element; parse a number of mesh elements syntax element specifying a number of mesh elements described in the mesh box; parse an explicit mesh identifier syntax element indicating whether a mesh element identifier syntax element is present in the mesh box; and parse the mesh element identifier syntax element, which specifies an identifier to uniquely identify a mesh element within the mesh box, by using a value of the explicit mesh identifier syntax element for the number of mesh elements. A device comprising one or more processors configured to: signal a number of mesh elements syntax element specifying a number of mesh elements described in a mesh box; signal an explicit mesh identifier syntax element indicating whether a mesh element identifier syntax element is present in the mesh box; and signal the mesh element identifier syntax element, which specifies an identifier to uniquely identify a mesh element within the mesh box, by using a value of the explicit mesh identifier syntax element for the number of mesh elements, wherein the mesh box specifies a three dimensional mesh consisting of one or more mesh element.
